title: Your First Test
intro: It's time to write a test from scratch. You have a function that validates email addresses, and you need to write tests to make sure it works correctly. Let's set up Jest and write your first real test.

teach.title: Your First Test = Your First Recipe
teach.body: Writing your first test is like following a recipe for the first time. The ingredients are simple (a function to test, some inputs, and expected outputs), and the steps are always the same (arrange, act, assert). Once you've done it once, you can do it for any function.

teach.title: Setting Up Jest
teach.body:
To start testing with Jest, you need:

1. Install Jest: `npm install --save-dev jest`
2. Add a test script to package.json: `"test": "jest"`
3. Create a test file with `.test.js` extension
4. Run tests with: `npm test`

Jest automatically finds and runs all files ending in `.test.js`.

teach.title: The Test File Structure
teach.annotation: 'describe' groups related tests together. Each 'test' checks one specific behavior. This structure makes test output easy to read.

teach.body: Set up Jest with `npm install --save-dev jest`, write test files ending in `.test.js`, group tests with `describe()`, and run them with `npm test`.

steps.instruction: First, let's look at the email validation function we need to test.
steps.hint: Use 'cat' to view the email.js source file.

steps.instruction: Install Jest as a development dependency so we can write and run tests.
steps.output:
added 1 package in 2s

1 package is looking for funding
  run `npm fund` for details
steps.hint: Install 'jest' as a dev dependency using npm.

steps.instruction: Now run the tests. Jest will look for test files automatically.
steps.output:
PASS  tests/email.test.js
  isValidEmail
    ✓ returns true for valid email (2ms)
    ✓ returns false for email without @ (1ms)
    ✓ returns false for email without domain (1ms)
    ✓ returns false for null input (1ms)
    ✓ returns false for empty string (1ms)

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       5 passed, 5 total
Time:        0.6s
steps.hint: Run the tests with npm test.

steps.instruction: All 5 tests pass! Let's run the tests in watch mode so they re-run automatically whenever you change a file.
steps.output:
PASS  tests/email.test.js
  isValidEmail
    ✓ returns true for valid email (2ms)
    ✓ returns false for email without @ (1ms)
    ✓ returns false for email without domain (1ms)
    ✓ returns false for null input (1ms)
    ✓ returns false for empty string (1ms)

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       5 passed, 5 total

Watch Usage
 › Press f to run only failed tests.
 › Press q to quit watch mode.
 › Press Enter to trigger a test run.
steps.hint: Add the --watch flag to run tests automatically when files change.

exercise.completionMessage: You just set up and ran a real test suite! Watch mode is especially powerful -- save a file and your tests run instantly. This tight feedback loop is why developers love automated testing.
